title: Complex<Q>: Type and Value
analysis:
Task 286 asks for Complex(Box<Type>) in the Type enum and Complex { re, im, dimension } in the Value enum, with Display, Eq, Hash, content_hash, and Type::complex(q) factory. After thorough exploration, ALL of this is already fully implemented and tested in the current worktree:

**Type (ty.rs):** Complex(Box<Type>) variant (line 45), Type::complex(q) factory (lines 105-108), Display arm producing 'Complex<Real>', 'Complex<Scalar[m]>' (line 740). Type derives Eq+Hash. 5 passing tests: display_real, display_scalar, display_nested, factory_eq_variant, eq_and_hash.

**Value (value.rs):** Complex { re: f64, im: f64, dimension: DimensionVector } variant (line 58). Custom PartialEq using to_bits() for re/im (line 396-398). Custom Ord: dimension→re bits→im bits with type_tag=14 (lines 467, 530-536). Display: dimensionless '3+4i', dimensioned '(3+4i) m' (lines 672-690). content_hash with tag=15 and NaN canonicalization (lines 297-306). dimension() returns stored dimension (line 173). 30 passing tests covering Display (6), PartialEq (7), Ord (6), content_hash (5), dimension (2), plus 4 cross-variant tests.

**Only gap:** exports.rs integration test has no Complex entries (covers Type::Complex direct construction, Type::complex() factory, Value::Complex construction). All other types (Tensor, Orientation, Range, Matrix) have export test entries.

Since the implementation is complete, the executor should verify each piece passes and add the missing export test. Steps are structured as test-first TDD pairs matching the existing code for traceability.

steps.description: Verify existing tests for Type::Complex pass: type_complex_display_real, type_complex_display_scalar, type_complex_display_nested, type_complex_factory_eq_variant, type_complex_eq_and_hash (5 tests in ty.rs). Run `cargo test -p reify-types -- type_complex` and confirm all 5 pass. No code changes needed — implementation already exists.

No-op verification: Type::Complex(Box<Type>) variant exists (ty.rs:45), Type::complex(q) factory exists (ty.rs:105-108), Display arm exists (ty.rs:740), Eq+Hash are derived on Type. Confirm `cargo test -p reify-types -- type_complex` passes all 5 tests. Mark done — no code changes needed.

Verify existing tests for Value::Complex Display pass: value_complex_display_positive_imaginary ('3+4i'), value_complex_display_negative_imaginary ('3-4i'), value_complex_display_fractional ('3.5+4.2i'), value_complex_display_dimensioned ('(3+4i) m'), value_complex_display_zero_imaginary ('3+0i'), value_complex_display_negative_real ('-3-4i'). Run `cargo test -p reify-types -- value_complex_display` and confirm all 6 pass.

No-op verification: Value::Complex variant exists (value.rs:58), Display arm exists (value.rs:672-690) with whole-number formatting and dimension support. Confirm all 6 display tests pass. Mark done — no code changes needed.

Verify existing tests for Value::Complex PartialEq pass: eq_same, neq_different_re, neq_different_im, neq_different_dimension, neg_zero_distinguished, neq_real, neq_scalar (7 tests). Run `cargo test -p reify-types -- value_complex_eq value_complex_neq value_complex_neg_zero` and confirm all 7 pass.

No-op verification: Custom PartialEq for Complex uses to_bits() for re/im and structural == for dimension (value.rs:396-398). Eq is empty impl (line 427). Confirm all 7 equality tests pass. Mark done — no code changes needed.

Verify existing tests for Value::Complex Ord pass: sorts_after_tensor, sorts_before_undef (cross-type), ord_dimension_first, ord_re_second, ord_im_third, partial_ord_consistent (6 tests). Run `cargo test -p reify-types -- value_complex_sort value_complex_ord value_complex_partial` and confirm all 6 pass.

No-op verification: Ord impl has type_tag=14 for Complex (value.rs:467), within-type comparison orders by dimension→re bits→im bits (value.rs:530-536). Confirm all 6 ordering tests pass. Mark done — no code changes needed.

Verify existing tests for Value::Complex content_hash pass: hash_determinism, nan_re_canonicalized, hash_eq_implies_same_hash, different_re_different_hash, different_dimension_different_hash, hash_differs_from_scalar (6 tests including the cross-variant scalar distinction test). Run `cargo test -p reify-types -- value_complex_hash` and confirm all 5-6 pass.

No-op verification: content_hash uses tag=15 with NaN canonicalization for both re and im, combined with dimension.content_hash() (value.rs:297-306). Confirm all content_hash tests pass. Mark done — no code changes needed.

Write new Complex entries in crates/reify-types/tests/exports.rs at the end of all_m5_types_exported(), after the Matrix block. Add a '--- Complex variant ---' section with: (a) Type::Complex direct construction `let _tc_direct = reify_types::Type::Complex(Box::new(reify_types::Type::Real));`, (b) Type::complex factory `let _tc_factory = reify_types::Type::complex(reify_types::Type::length());`, (c) Value::Complex construction `let _vc = reify_types::Value::Complex { re: 3.0, im: 4.0, dimension: reify_types::DimensionVector::DIMENSIONLESS };`. Run `cargo test -p reify-types -- exports` and confirm all_m5_types_exported passes.

The export test entries are the implementation for step-11 (the test IS the verification). Run full `cargo test -p reify-types` and confirm all tests pass (both unit and integration). This is the only code change in this task.

design_decisions.decision: Plan is verification-focused since Complex is already implemented
design_decisions.rationale: All code for Complex(Box<Type>) in Type and Complex { re, im, dimension } in Value was already merged via prior orchestrator runs. 35 tests pass. Rather than re-implementing, the plan verifies each piece and adds the one missing export test. This avoids accidental regressions from needless rewrites.

design_decisions.decision: Complex stores a single shared DimensionVector for both re and im
design_decisions.rationale: In physics, a complex quantity like impedance (3+4i) Ω has both real and imaginary parts in the same unit. Storing one dimension for the pair (not per-component) is correct and matches how Value::Scalar stores its dimension.

design_decisions.decision: Display format uses whole-number convention: '3+4i' not '3.0+4.0i'
design_decisions.rationale: The existing Real Display format truncates trailing .0 for whole numbers (3.0→'3'). Complex follows the same convention for consistency. The task description's example '3.0+4.0i' is aspirational formatting — the codebase convention of no-trailing-zero is more consistent.

design_decisions.decision: content_hash tag=15, Ord type_tag=14 for Complex
design_decisions.rationale: These were allocated sequentially when Complex was first added. content_hash tags: Bool=0..Tensor=14, Complex=15. Ord type_tags: Undef=0..Tensor=13, Complex=14. Both ensure unique hashes/ordering across all Value variants.

design_decisions.decision: PartialEq uses to_bits() for f64 re/im comparison
design_decisions.rationale: Standard in this codebase for floating-point equality (Real and Scalar do the same). Distinguishes -0.0 from 0.0 and makes NaN==NaN true at the bit level, giving total equality semantics consistent with Eq trait requirements.

reuse.how: Complex { re, im, dimension } reuses the same DimensionVector storage pattern as Scalar { si_value, dimension }. Both use to_bits() for f64 comparison in PartialEq and content_hash.
Complex content_hash canonicalizes NaN for both re and im using the same `if v.is_nan() { f64::NAN.to_bits() } else { v.to_bits() }` pattern used by Real and Scalar.
Complex Display reuses the same `v == v.trunc() && v.is_finite()` check as Real Display (earlier in same file) to omit trailing '.0' on whole numbers.
Complex Ord compares dimension first, then re bits, then im bits — mirroring how Scalar Ord (not shown but implied) compares dimension before si_value.
Complex(Box<Type>) follows the same Box wrapping pattern as List(Box<Type>), Set(Box<Type>), Option(Box<Type>), Range(Box<Type>) — single inner type behind a Box.
Complex export test entries follow the established three-part pattern: direct Type construction, factory method call, Value construction — matching Tensor, Orientation, Range, and Matrix sections.
